2026-04-16T01:51:29.0243080Z ##[group]Run # Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)
2026-04-16T01:51:29.0243645Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-16T01:51:29.0244081Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-16T01:51:29.0244441Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-16T01:51:29.0244745Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-16T01:51:29.0244975Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-16T01:51:29.0245129Z [36;1mPORT=3001[0m
2026-04-16T01:51:29.0245306Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-16T01:51:29.0245469Z [36;1m# Set outputs for next steps[0m
2026-04-16T01:51:29.0245690Z [36;1m{[0m
2026-04-16T01:51:29.0245884Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_api_key=${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-16T01:51:29.0246163Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_port=${PORT}"[0m
2026-04-16T01:51:29.0246414Z [36;1m} >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"[0m

2026-04-16T01:52:51.0032294Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-16T01:52:34.24861406Z","direction":"OUT","type":"REQUEST","server_id":"github","method":"tools/call","payload":{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"arguments":{"issue_number":"58197","method":"get","owner":"Azure","repo":"azure-sdk-for-net"},"name":"issue_read"}}}
2026-04-16T01:52:51.0052410Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-16T01:52:34.508883554Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":58197,\"title\":\"[Mgmt Generator] @@hierarchyBuilding causes backward-compat ModelFactory overload to pass arguments in wrong order\",\"body\":\"## Bug: `@@hierarchyBuilding` causes backward-compat ModelFactory overload to pass arguments in wrong order\\n\\n### Description\\n\\nWhen `@@hierarchyBuilding` is applied to a non-ARM model (e.g., a Patch model) to restore a `TrackedResourceData` base type, the generator creates a backward-compat ModelFactory overload that passes arguments in the **internal constructor parameter order** instead of the **public method parameter order**.\\n\\n### Minimal Reproduction\\n\\nAdd to an existing test project (e.g., `Mgmt-TypeSpec`):\\n\\n**TypeSpec model:**\\n```typespec\\nmodel PatchModel {\\n  location?: string;\\n\\n  @visibility(Lifecycle.Read)\\n  id?: string;\\n\\n  @visibility(Lifecycle.Read)\\n  name?: string;\\n\\n  @visibility(Lifecycle.Read)\\n  type?: string;\\n\\n  tags?: Record;\\n\\n  properties?: PatchProperties;\\n}\\n\\nmodel PatchProperties {\\n  size?: int64;\\n  qosType?: string;\\n  coolAccess?: boolean;\\n}\\n```\\n\\n**client.tsp decorators:**\\n```typespec\\n@@alternateType(PatchModel.id, armResourceIdentifier, \\u0026#34;csharp\\u0026#34;);\\n@@alternateType(PatchModel.location, azureLocation, \\u0026#34;csharp\\u0026#34;);\\n#suppress \\u0026#34;@azure-tools/typespec-azure-core/no-legacy-usage\\u0026#34; \\u0026#34;Restore TrackedResourceData base type\\u0026#34;\\n@@hierarchyBuilding(PatchModel, Azure.ResourceManager.Foundations.TrackedResource, \\u0026#34;csharp\\u0026#34;);\\n```\\n\\n### Expected Behavior\\n\\nThe backward-compat ModelFactory overload should delegate correctly:\\n```csharp\\n// Backward-compat overload (no defaults)\\npublic static PatchModel PatchModel(ResourceIdentifier id, string name, ResourceType resourceType, SystemData systemData, IDictionary tags, AzureLocation location, long? size, string qosType, bool? coolAccess)\\n{\\n    // Should match parameter NAMES of the primary overload\\n    return PatchModel(id: id, name: name, resourceType: resourceType, systemData: systemData, tags: tags, location: location, size: size, qosType: qosType, coolAccess: coolAccess);\\n}\\n```\\n\\n### Actual Behavior\\n\\nThe generator produces:\\n```csharp\\n// Generated backward-compat overload — WRONG argument order\\npublic static PatchModel PatchModel(ResourceIdentifier id, string name, ResourceType resourceType, SystemData systemData, IDictionary tags, AzureLocation location, long? size, string qosType, bool? coolAccess)\\n{\\n    // Passes args in INTERNAL CONSTRUCTOR order, not public method order\\n    return PatchModel(resourceType, systemData, location, id, name, tags, size, qosType, coolAccess);\\n}\\n```\\n\\nThis causes compile error `CS1503` because `resourceType` (a `ResourceType`) is passed where `id` (a `ResourceIdentifier`) is expected, etc.\\n\\n### Root Cause\\n\\nThe internal constructor parameter order differs from the public ModelFactory method parameter order:\\n- **Internal constructor**: `(ResourceType resourceType, SystemData systemData, IDictionary additionalBinaryDataProperties, AzureLocation location, ResourceIdentifier id, string name, IDictionary tags, PatchProperties properties)`\\n- **Public method**: `(ResourceIdentifier id, string name, ResourceType resourceType, SystemData systemData, IDictionary tags, AzureLocation location, ...)`\\n\\nThe generator\\u0026#39;s argument-forwarding logic for the backward-compat overload appears to use the internal constructor\\u0026#39;s parameter ordering instead of matching by parameter name against the target (primary) method.\\n\\n### Impact\\n\\nThis blocks using `@@hierarchyBuilding` on Patch models during MPG migration (AutoRest → TypeSpec). Discovered during NetApp SDK migration.\\n\\n### Workaround\\n\\nNone — the backward-compat overload is in generated code and can\\u0026#39;t be overridden in a partial class (same static method signature). The only workaround is to not use `@@hierarchyBuilding` on the affected model and accept the `CannotRemoveBaseTypeOrInterface` API compat violation.\\n\",\"state\":\"open\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/58197\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"live1206\",\"id\":5196139,\"profile_url\":\"https://github.com/live1206\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/5196139?v=4\"},\"author_association\":\"MEMBER\",\"labels\":[\"bug\"],\"reactions\":{\"total_count\":1,\"+1\":0,\"-1\":0,\"laugh\":0,\"confused\":0,\"heart\":0,\"hooray\":0,\"rocket\":0,\"eyes\":1},\"created_at\":\"2026-04-16T01:49:50Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-04-16T01:49:50Z\"}","type":"text"}]}}}
2026-04-16T01:52:51.0069891Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-16T01:52:34.521813335Z","direction":"OUT","type":"REQUEST","server_id":"github","method":"tools/call","payload":{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"arguments":{"issue_number":58197,"method":"get","owner":"Azure","repo":"azure-sdk-for-net"},"name":"issue_read"}}}
2026-04-16T01:52:51.0089985Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-16T01:52:34.675014221Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":58197,\"title\":\"[Mgmt Generator] @@hierarchyBuilding causes backward-compat ModelFactory overload to pass arguments in wrong order\",\"body\":\"## Bug: `@@hierarchyBuilding` causes backward-compat ModelFactory overload to pass arguments in wrong order\\n\\n### Description\\n\\nWhen `@@hierarchyBuilding` is applied to a non-ARM model (e.g., a Patch model) to restore a `TrackedResourceData` base type, the generator creates a backward-compat ModelFactory overload that passes arguments in the **internal constructor parameter order** instead of the **public method parameter order**.\\n\\n### Minimal Reproduction\\n\\nAdd to an existing test project (e.g., `Mgmt-TypeSpec`):\\n\\n**TypeSpec model:**\\n```typespec\\nmodel PatchModel {\\n  location?: string;\\n\\n  @visibility(Lifecycle.Read)\\n  id?: string;\\n\\n  @visibility(Lifecycle.Read)\\n  name?: string;\\n\\n  @visibility(Lifecycle.Read)\\n  type?: string;\\n\\n  tags?: Record;\\n\\n  properties?: PatchProperties;\\n}\\n\\nmodel PatchProperties {\\n  size?: int64;\\n  qosType?: string;\\n  coolAccess?: boolean;\\n}\\n```\\n\\n**client.tsp decorators:**\\n```typespec\\n@@alternateType(PatchModel.id, armResourceIdentifier, \\u0026#34;csharp\\u0026#34;);\\n@@alternateType(PatchModel.location, azureLocation, \\u0026#34;csharp\\u0026#34;);\\n#suppress \\u0026#34;@azure-tools/typespec-azure-core/no-legacy-usage\\u0026#34; \\u0026#34;Restore TrackedResourceData base type\\u0026#34;\\n@@hierarchyBuilding(PatchModel, Azure.ResourceManager.Foundations.TrackedResource, \\u0026#34;csharp\\u0026#34;);\\n```\\n\\n### Expected Behavior\\n\\nThe backward-compat ModelFactory overload should delegate correctly:\\n```csharp\\n// Backward-compat overload (no defaults)\\npublic static PatchModel PatchModel(ResourceIdentifier id, string name, ResourceType resourceType, SystemData systemData, IDictionary tags, AzureLocation location, long? size, string qosType, bool? coolAccess)\\n{\\n    // Should match parameter NAMES of the primary overload\\n    return PatchModel(id: id, name: name, resourceType: resourceType, systemData: systemData, tags: tags, location: location, size: size, qosType: qosType, coolAccess: coolAccess);\\n}\\n```\\n\\n### Actual Behavior\\n\\nThe generator produces:\\n```csharp\\n// Generated backward-compat overload — WRONG argument order\\npublic static PatchModel PatchModel(ResourceIdentifier id, string name, ResourceType resourceType, SystemData systemData, IDictionary tags, AzureLocation location, long? size, string qosType, bool? coolAccess)\\n{\\n    // Passes args in INTERNAL CONSTRUCTOR order, not public method order\\n    return PatchModel(resourceType, systemData, location, id, name, tags, size, qosType, coolAccess);\\n}\\n```\\n\\nThis causes compile error `CS1503` because `resourceType` (a `ResourceType`) is passed where `id` (a `ResourceIdentifier`) is expected, etc.\\n\\n### Root Cause\\n\\nThe internal constructor parameter order differs from the public ModelFactory method parameter order:\\n- **Internal constructor**: `(ResourceType resourceType, SystemData systemData, IDictionary additionalBinaryDataProperties, AzureLocation location, ResourceIdentifier id, string name, IDictionary tags, PatchProperties properties)`\\n- **Public method**: `(ResourceIdentifier id, string name, ResourceType resourceType, SystemData systemData, IDictionary tags, AzureLocation location, ...)`\\n\\nThe generator\\u0026#39;s argument-forwarding logic for the backward-compat overload appears to use the internal constructor\\u0026#39;s parameter ordering instead of matching by parameter name against the target (primary) method.\\n\\n### Impact\\n\\nThis blocks using `@@hierarchyBuilding` on Patch models during MPG migration (AutoRest → TypeSpec). Discovered during NetApp SDK migration.\\n\\n### Workaround\\n\\nNone — the backward-compat overload is in generated code and can\\u0026#39;t be overridden in a partial class (same static method signature). The only workaround is to not use `@@hierarchyBuilding` on the affected model and accept the `CannotRemoveBaseTypeOrInterface` API compat violation.\\n\",\"state\":\"open\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/58197\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"live1206\",\"id\":5196139,\"profile_url\":\"https://github.com/live1206\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/5196139?v=4\"},\"author_association\":\"MEMBER\",\"labels\":[\"bug\"],\"reactions\":{\"total_count\":1,\"+1\":0,\"-1\":0,\"laugh\":0,\"confused\":0,\"heart\":0,\"hooray\":0,\"rocket\":0,\"eyes\":1},\"created_at\":\"2026-04-16T01:49:50Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-04-16T01:49:50Z\"}","type":"text"}]}}}
